title: Criminal intimidation
content: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do... commits criminal intimidation.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A threatens to burn Z's house if Z does not drop a lawsuit against A.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A has committed criminal intimidation under Section 503.

footnotes.text: The Mental State: Physical violence does not need to actually occur. The crime is fully complete the moment a threat is communicated with the intent to cause psychological alarm or force an action.

title: Punishment for criminal intimidation
content: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both...

illumination_lamp.scenario: If the threat in Section 503 is to cause death or grievous hurt, the court may impose a more severe punishment than the standard two-year limit, as specified in the extended provisions of Section 506.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: The sentencing court has judicial discretion to enhance penalties based on the severity of the threatened injury.

sentencing_ladder.penalty: Up to 2 years imprisonment, or fine, or both

title: Misconduct in public by a drunken person
content: Whoever, in a state of intoxication, appears in any public place... and there conducts himself in such a manner as to cause annoyance to any person, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to twenty-four hours, or with fine... or with both.

sentencing_ladder.penalty: Up to 24 hours simple imprisonment, or fine, or both

illumination_lamp.scenario: A, in a state of intoxication, enters a crowded market and shouts offensive slurs at passersby, causing public annoyance.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A has committed the offence of misconduct in public under Section 510.

footnotes.text: The Smallest Statutory Penalty: This section holds the historical distinction of prescribing the shortest prison sentence available within the entire text of the penal code.
